column1: Luis Arraez leads the slate with a strong hit probability and a favorable home matchup against Matt Waldron, who has allowed steady contact this season. Arraez’s high recent hit rate and consistent performance in the two-hole make him a reliable option. Yandy Diaz also stands out, batting leadoff at home with a solid track record and facing a hittable lefty in Patrick Corbin. Xavier Edwards, Bo Bichette, and Bobby Witt Jr. offer additional stability, each benefiting from platoon advantages and soft pitching matchups.
